channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: INTERVIEW: ILL Developers Don’t Want to ‘Overly Punish Players’ With Chilling Horror Game
item.content:encoded:
ILL is shaping up to be one of the most violent games ever made. Every time Team Clout and Mundfish publish a new trailer, audiences stare in shock at the brutality before them. Naturally, most people are pairing that level of violence with a difficulty scale that they imagine will be pretty punishing, but that might not be the case.

In a recent interview with ILL’s developers, I learned exactly how difficult the game is set to be. It’s certainly not going to be easy, but the gratuitous violence isn’t being met with unnecessarily tough gameplay.

How Hard Will ILL Be?

ILL is a chilling game that boasts disturbing and hyper-realistic body damage, physics, and mutations, plunging players into a horrifying Soviet-era facility packed to the rafters with enemies known as Aberrations. It’s not for those with a weak temperament, as every trailer we’ve seen professes just how gory and shocking this title will be.

It makes sense when you understand that it’s from a team well-versed in cinematic horror, with credits spanning Until Dawn, IT: Welcome to Derry, V/H/S/Beyond, Longlegs, and Azrael, amongst others.

  • RELATED: ILL Could Be The Most Violent Game Ever

Fearing that ILL would be an oppressive and harrowing game to experience in terms of difficulty, I brought up the game’s learning curve when I spoke to the developers at Team Clout. I asked straight up, is this game as hard as it looks?

The team explained:

When it comes to difficulty, we don’t want to overly punish players, but we also don’t want them to feel like an unstoppable action hero who can just brute-force through every obstacle. Players will need to carefully and tactically manage their resources, which will be incredibly scarce, and makeshift melee weapons, like pipes ripped straight from the walls, will degrade and break over time.

It’s a balance, and hopefully, it works well in the final product. I’m game for a challenge, but at the same time, I despise when games crank up the difficulty just for the sake of it. I should be scared when playing a game like ILL, not frustrated.

The team expanded on the game’s loop:

You’ll have to think on your feet: deciding which resources to use now, what to save for later, and assessing the specific types of monsters in front of you to properly leverage the environment and your position. So yes, players will need to stay sharp and adapt, but we don’t want the experience to be frustratingly difficult.

First and foremost, we want the action to be fun, even amidst the unfolding bloody nightmare. For us, it’s all about striking that perfect balance between survival horror and action. 

ILL has far surpassed a million wishlists, and it’s fast becoming one of the most anticipated horror games ever made. Are you looking forward to ILL? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that a Wolverine actor will be in GTA 6, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Fortnite Adding Five New Sprites & Soccer Stadium in Next Update
item.content:encoded:
A fresh influx of content is coming to Fortnite this week, and while the major headline will be the transition into Fortnite OG Season 9, there are also new features coming to the main Battle Royale mode.

After two weeks so far in the Runners season of Chapter 7, Season 3, where players have been frantically collecting and extracting Sprites, a new drop is adding more to the roster, alongside a new stadium POI.

Fortnite Adding New Sprites & Soccer Stadium

Epic Games already teased the addition of new Sprites coming later this week, but Loolo_WRLD on X has shared a full breakdown on each of them and the effects they provide.

The Striker Sprite, with a football aesthetic that coincides with the World Cup taking place in North America, is unlocked by scoring a goal at the new Soccer Pitch POI, meaning it will have a different method to obtain than others.

Additionally, the new Boss Sprite also has a different mechanic for earning it, as it’s dropped by defeating any boss this season, and it seems like neither of those Sprites will be available from standard means, like looting, though you will be able to collect it from enemies that have been eliminated, if they’re carrying it.

  • Related: What Time Does Fortnite OG Season 9 Release? Exact Start Time & Date

Meanwhile, the Fishy Sprite, which includes swim speed and provides a movement speed boost when taking damage, the Aura Sprite, which provides a Shock Rock charge when you deal enough damage, and the Grim Reaper Sprite, which results in anyone who attacks you getting marked, are also being added to the loot pool.

At the time of writing, it remains to be seen what rarities the new Sprites will be, and whether they will be available in other variants like Gold, Gummy, and Galaxy.

With Chapter 7, Season 3 due to continue until mid-August, there’s still plenty of time for more Sprites and variants to be added to the loot pool.

How close are you to a complete Sprite collection? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For your next read, take a look at how players are spending real money on Sprites, and don’t forget to sign up for the Insider Gaming newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: God Of War Laufey Actress: ‘This Is Just The Beginning’
item.content:encoded:
While the Norse saga may have seemingly concluded with God of War Ragnarök, it’s clear Santa Monica isn’t finished exploring that corner of the universe just yet. God of War: Laufey marks the next chapter for the franchise, and according to its lead actress, it’s only the start of something much bigger.

Announced during the June 2026 PlayStation State of Play, God of War: Laufey closed the showcase with more than 20 minutes of gameplay footage. The title shifts the spotlight away from Kratos and onto his late wife, Faye, as she journeys through the mysterious afterlife realm known as the Everywhen.

God Of War Laufey Expands The GoW Universe

Speaking with Comic Games Magazine, Deborah Ann Woll discussed what God of War: Laufey means for the future of the series, as well as the responsibility that comes with leading the next era of the franchise.

‘It’s a privilege,’ Woll explained, adding that she feels ‘nothing but gratitude and responsibility’ for taking on such a significant role.

When discussing the future of God of War, Woll revealed that Laufey represents a new direction for the franchise, while making it clear that Kratos’ story isn’t over. Instead, Santa Monica Studio is looking to broaden the scope of the universe and explore stories beyond the Ghost of Sparta’s perspective.

We want this to be huge. We want you to feel like there’s such an untapped universe. And this is just the beginning of a step into some of that.

Woll went on to explain that Laufey will further expand the mythology established in the Norse saga. The story runs alongside the events of 2018’s God of War, offering a new perspective on key moments and showing how Faye’s journey intertwines with Kratos’ own adventure.

  • Related: God Of War Laufey Is Ten Years In The Making

It might star a new protagonist and cast of characters, but everything is pointing to Laufey being as God of War as ever before. Check out the reveal trailer below for a refresher of what’s to come.

What do you think of God of War: Laufey? Are you excited to see how the narrative unfolds? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the new Marvel ARPG ‘Project Comet’ leaks that surfaced online, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: Team Vitality Bench Humanoid Before LEC Summer Split
item.content:encoded:
Team Vitality has announced that Czech midlaner Marek ‘Humanoid’ Brázda has been benched ahead of the LEC Summer Split.

The experienced League of Legends pro looked like he had made the difference for the team, finishing first in the Spring regular season.

Yet, Vitality then crashed and burned in the Spring Playoffs and were eliminated early from the Esports World Cup qualifiers as well.

This is now the second year in a row that Humanoid has been benched before the Summer Split, as Fnatic did the same in 2025.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Vitality LEC Woes

Other than a Week 1 loss to Karmine Corp, Team Vitality didn’t lose a single regular season series, finishing 8-1 before the Playoffs.

Yet, in the Playoffs, Vitality didn’t win a single game, losing 3-0 to Movistar KOI and then GIANTX. The first seeds were the first team eliminated.

And they had missed on EWC after a shock 2-0 loss to Shifters in the online qualifiers.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

After taking a break during the Summer Split last year, it had looked like Humanoid was returning to form.

Humanoid was the first-ever European player to make Worlds three years in a row without playing for G2 or Fnatic, doing so with Splyce and MAD Lions.

However, he has also always been known as a player who is very inconsistent and prone to random deaths, and that seems to still be the case much later into his career.

Meanwhile, Vitality as an organisation hasn’t attended Worlds since 2018 and is now taking drastic measures, dropping assistant coach Luka ‘Lukezy’ Trumbić too.

Who is Replacing Humanoid?

According to reports by Sheep Esports, Vitality are considering a Korean replacement for their LEC mid lane position.

While no names have been confirmed, it’s said that the organisation also looked at France’s LFL and, specifically, Solary’s Kang ‘Jool’ Dong-su and Galions’ Šimon ‘OMON’ Řiháč.

Similarly, Fnatic is looking to Korea for a new LEC toplaner after benching Panagiotis ‘Empyros’ Tantis.

Assumedly, both are looking in Korea’s second tier, the LCK Challengers League.

Will a Korean pro help Team Vitality challenge the current big three? Share your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, check out the LoL MSI 2026 Play-In draw.

MOBILE APP

item.title: God Of War Laufey Is Ten Years In The Making
item.content:encoded:
God of War: Laufey was officially revealed during the June 2026 PlayStation State of Play, closing out the showcase with more than 20 minutes of gameplay footage. While rumors had pointed to its existence beforehand, the reveal still came as a surprise to many fans.

The game marks a significant departure from Kratos’ Norse saga, shifting the spotlight to Faye, also known as Laufey. While some may view it as a fresh start for the franchise, new comments from lead actress Deborah Ann Woll suggest the project has been part of Santa Monica Studio’s plans for far longer than anyone expected.

God Of War Laufey Was Seemingly Always The Plan

Speaking with Comic Games Magazine, Woll reflected on finally being able to discuss the game publicly after keeping it under wraps for so long.

‘I’ve known about this for nearly ten years,’ Woll said, explaining how difficult it had been to stay silent about the project ahead of its reveal.

According to Woll, the God of War: Laufey was first pitched to her in 2018, the same year God of War launched, though she added that the concept had already been established internally before then.

  • Related: Sony Emails Now Teasing That God of War Laufey is ‘Coming Soon’

Woll recalled that when head of creative Cory Barlog brought her in to discuss Ragnarök, he already had artwork for God of War: Laufey displayed in his office. The poster reportedly featured both Faye and the mysterious new companion character, Phranque.

Phranque, the sentient gelatinous cube introduced during the game’s reveal, quickly became one of the showcase’s most talked-about surprises. While some fans assumed the character was simply comic relief, Woll revealed that Phranque has been part of the project’s vision from the very beginning and is ‘deeply a part of the lore.’

How that presents itself in the overarching narrative remains to be seen, but it’s obvious that some serious background planning has gone into the franchise, and some fans think it could even be coming out soon. Check out the trailer below for a reminder of what’s next for God of War.

Are you excited to play God of War Laufey? What else do you think Santa Monica has planned? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about the new Marvel ARPG ‘Project Comet’ leaks that surfaced online, and don’t forget to sign up for our Newsletter.

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

item.title: The Leaked GTA 6 Price Online Isn’t What You Think
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto 6 details are expected to come from Rockstar Games any day now, but the company hasn’t revealed the game’s price tag, although an online report claims it’s already available.

Get ready for a whirlwind of GTA 6 information this month. We’ve already had a new look at the GTA 6 Vice City skyline, and the game’s price is stirring things up. Over 60% of gamers think GTA 6 will cost more than $70, and leaked prices suggest this will be the case.

How much will it actually be?

Grand Theft Auto 6 Price is a Placeholder

Billbil-kun is a self-described private investigator and addressed claims that the GTA 6 price has been found online:

‘Looks like many people (including Rockstar) are trying to disturb my holidays. Those SKUs (stock keeping units) are just placeholders because EAN codes (like UPC in US), aren’t belonging to Take2 traditional games prefixes. So those prices are random ones.’

In short, these GTA 6 prices you’re seeing online are guesses, and nothing more for now.

  • More Insider Gaming: GTA 6 ‘Cover Girl’ Has Responded to Claim She’s The Model

The truth is, absolutely no one in the world knows how much GTA 6 is going to cost. We will all find out at the same time on June 25, unless the real figures do leak before then.

Anything GTA 6-related is enough to poke a combustible community desperate for any sliver of info. I mean, the GTA 6 release date disappeared, and everyone started losing their minds!

While we do wait for the actual GTA 6 price, as well as pre-order news, and Trailer 3, fill the void by rewatching Trailer 2 for the hundredth time.

How much will GTA 6 cost? Let us know your answer through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about Congratulations On Your Purchase costing $1,000 on Steam, seriously, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

item.title: Congratulations On Your Purchase Costs $1,000 on Steam, Seriously
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve got a spare thousand dollars just sitting around, then why not contribute to one of 2026’s most bizarre games: Congratulations On Your Purchase. Seriously, what is happening?

Congratulations On Your Purchase has appeared on Steam, and understandably, everyone is simultaneously confused, shocked, angry, baffled, amused, and wondering what’s real and what isn’t.

‘The Most Expensive Game On Steam’ is Congratulations On Your Purchase

To catch you up, here’s Steam‘s official description for Congratulations On Your Purchase:

‘The most expensive game on Steam. A palace, a red carpet, paparazzi, and a wall where you leave your name — visible to every owner who comes after you. Ten minutes. The price is not a mistake. It is the point.’

Not only does this short description get to the point, but it tells you EVERYTHING that happens in this game, and if I’m being cynical, I’m not sure what it is, because it isn’t a game.

  • More Insider Gaming: Devil May Cry Remake ‘Pretty Far Along’

The developer’s justification continues:

‘You paid for this. Not accidentally. Not on impulse. You saw the price. You read the description. And then you bought it anyway. Welcome.

There is no combat. There are no enemies. There are no quests, no skill trees, no loot boxes — well, there is one box, but it contains only the feeling of having arrived somewhere important. You will walk. You will look. You will leave something behind. That is all. That is everything.’

For $999.99 or £748, exactly, you basically pay to have your name on a wall. Once you’ve done this, you’ve basically seen everything that Congratulations On Your Purchase has to offer.

Even more eye-watering is the fact that the Steam page mentions: ‘Some of the store page artwork for this game was created with the assistance of generative AI image tools.

I have a feeling the discourse on Congratulations On Your Purchase is going to be fascinating, so keep your ears open for more on this game…experience… experiment?

If you missed it, I Am Rich was another similar game that operated in a familiar capacity to Congratulations On Your Purchase.

So, how many copies of Congratulations On Your Purchase are you picking up? Let us know through the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, read about The Last of Us 2 Multiplayer Mod release being announced, and don’t forget to sign up for our Insider Gaming Newsletter.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…
